summary: Near-complete decompilation of the vi1201v2 scene enter routine. The PR added a C body for un_80320A40_OnEnter, expanded contiguous data/string layout around un_80400304, typed the fog erase color as GXColor, and updated the header signature from UNK_T to void*. decomp-dev reported un_80320A40_OnEnter improving from 0.00% to 97.14%, with .sdata reaching 100%.

notable_changes: Added static HSD_JObj transform setter shims with matching asserts
Changed erase color handling to GXColor fields
Changed un_804D7028 extern type from u8 to GXColor
Changed un_80320A40_OnEnter declaration from UNK_T to void*

what_changed: Added includes for effect async/lib, ft_0C31, lbArchive/lb_00B0, baselib forward declarations, and toy/ty headers needed by the new scene setup body.

Replaced the standalone Vec3 un_80400304 with un_80400304_t containing player_spawn plus embedded assert and asset/symbol strings such as Vi1201v2.dat, visual1201v2Scene, TyKoopa.dat, ToyKoopaModel_TopN_joint, GmRgStnd.dat, and standScene.
Updated Player_80032768 to pass &un_80400304.player_spawn after the data symbol was widened into a struct.

Added globals for the additional scene descriptors, joint, fog color, state value, and two exported HSD_GObj pointers: un_804D7014, un_804D7020, un_804D7028, un_804D702C, un_804D7030, and un_804D7034.

Implemented un_80320A40_OnEnter: initializes effects/audio, reads input bytes for character/variant data, loads scene and model archives, creates camera/model/fog/light GObjs, sets up callbacks/procs, applies child joint translation/rotation/scale from un_803060BC, and restores the audio state at exit.

Added local HSD_JObjSetRotationY_2, HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z_2, and HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z_2 helpers that directly mutate JObj transform fields and call ftCo_800C6AFC unless JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT is set.
Changed un_803208F0 color handling from u8* byte indexing to GXColor* with r/g/b/a fields before calling HSD_SetEraseColor.
smart_moves: Modeling un_80400304 as a packed local struct preserved the existing player spawn data while exposing nearby embedded strings needed by offset-based references in un_80320A40_OnEnter.
Typing un_804D7028 as GXColor matched the fog color source and made HSD_SetEraseColor argument order explicit through colors->r, colors->g, colors->b, and colors->a.

The static *_2 JObj setter helpers reproduce assert strings, jobj.h line numbers, direct field writes, and matrix invalidation behavior, which is useful when ordinary library calls or macros do not produce matching code.
Using a char* base of &un_804002F8 plus numeric offsets kept the data-reference pattern visible for archive symbol loads and the quaternion assert string.
The header cleanup followed evidence from the implementation: void* for the raw scene argument and GXColor for the exported erase/fog color.
decomp_lessons: When a previously typed data symbol is followed by strings or other constants, widen it into a struct with named subfields rather than leaving adjacent data anonymous; update existing users to the relevant subfield.
Fixed-size char arrays can be a matching tactic for packed data blobs; future work should treat these as layout-sensitive data, not necessarily independent high-level strings.
Prefer concrete types such as GXColor once the implementation shows field-level usage, especially for globals exported through headers.
Scene OnEnter matches often need broad subsystem includes because they touch archives, camera, GObj setup, effects, audio, player setup, fog, lights, and model joints in one routine.
For inline HSD/JObj APIs, local helper clones with exact asserts and flag checks can be a practical bridge when matching compiler output.
Bot progress reports should be recorded with nuance: this PR greatly improved function/data matching but still left un_80320A40_OnEnter at 97.14% and reported a small .sdata2 regression.
naming_conventions: Address-based unknown names were preserved: un_80320A40_OnEnter, un_80400304, un_804D7028, un_804D7030, and related symbols.
Scene callback suffixes follow existing convention: _OnEnter and _OnFrame.
Static matching helper clones use the original HSD-style name plus _2, for example HSD_JObjSetTranslateX_2 and HSD_JObjSetScaleZ_2.

Temporary struct fields use descriptive snake_case for embedded data roles: player_spawn, use_quaternion_assert, vi1201v2_dat, visual1201v2_scene, tykoopa_dat, toykoopa_model_topn_joint, gmrgstnd_dat, and stand_scene.
Asset string contents retain original game capitalization, such as TyKoopa.dat and ToyKoopaModel_TopN_joint.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Casts the raw OnEnter argument to u8* and reads input[0] and input[1].
Uses char* data = (char*) &un_804002F8 and offsets 0x40, 0x50, 0x64, 0x70, 0x8C, and 0x9C for lbArchive_LoadSymbols calls.
Quaternion assertion passes the message address as (char*) &un_804002F8 + 0x18.
JObj helper shims explicitly call __assert("jobj.h", line, "jobj") and test JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION or JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT before direct field writes.
Changed the erase-color match from colors[0..3] byte indexing to GXColor field access.
Uses callback/object setup casts such as GObj_SetupGXLinkMax(gobj, (void (*)(HSD_GObj*, int)) un_803208F0, 8).

review_feedback.note: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Reported .sdata as a new match for main/melee/vi/vi1201v2: +16 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.
Reported .data improvement to 98.80% and .sbss improvement to 91.67%, plus a .sdata2 regression of -2 bytes from 76.92% to 71.43%.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete the remaining mismatch in un_80320A40_OnEnter for vi1201v2.
Search for other vi scene PRs using packed data structs with embedded archive/symbol strings.
Audit whether HSD_JObjSet*_2 helper clones are still needed or can be replaced after broader matching improvements.
Check if the .sdata2 regression reported by decomp-dev in PR 2456 was later recovered.
Look for later naming/type refinements of un_80400304_t, un_804D702C, un_804D7030, and un_804D7034.
